items.correct: Output per unit of input (per worker, per hour) — measuring efficiency, not total output. A small efficient firm can be more productive than a large inefficient one.
items.misconception: Productivity is the total amount produced by a business.
items.random_wrong: Productivity is the number of employees a business has.
items.why: Total output and productivity are different. A factory of 100 workers producing 1000 units is less productive than 10 workers producing 200 units. The ratio matters, not the total.

items.correct: An arrangement where a franchisor grants a franchisee the right to use their brand, products and systems in exchange for fees and a share of revenue.
items.misconception: A franchise is when a business owns multiple branches itself.
items.random_wrong: A franchise is when employees buy shares in their company.
items.why: Owned chains are not franchises. A franchise involves separate ownership — McDonald's restaurants are mostly franchised (owned by individual operators), not owned by McDonald's Corporation.

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.
Correct answer: Just-in-time. “Job inventory tracking” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Continuous improvement. “Cost cutting” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If actual output is 800 and maximum is 1000, what is capacity utilisation (%)?
items.why: The real figure is 80 %. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
